tips.text: No new keys today. We drill the rhythm on a few plain words, so the hands stay even when the symbols arrive.
Keep your fingers home and your eyes on the screen. Type each word at one regular pace, no faster at the end than the start. Tempo before speed.

tips.title: Even strokes
tips.text: Hold a steady gap between keys and don't let it wander. Calm and regular beats hurried and rough. Even keystrokes read like clean copy.

tips.title: Rhythm: return
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: return return return. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.title: Rhythm: value
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: value value value. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.title: Rhythm: data
tips.hint: Repeat at one even pace: data data data. Hold the same beat from first key to last.

tips.text: Code punishes a ragged hand, where one slip means a broken line. The even rhythm you build on plain words is exactly what carries you cleanly through syntax.
Worked through every step? Open the trainer below and make one full run on the lesson text. That run saves your progress and unlocks the next lesson.

character_tips.anna: Real code rhythm — you can type a function now.
character_tips.maxim: This is how programmers type all day. Welcome to coding speed.
character_tips.knopych: CODE TYPING! Symbols + numbers + letters — full power! 💻
character_tips.klavochka: Code is like another language — and you speak it now! ✨
